summary: EC8 Phase 8c (Scheduling + Employee Portal) tested end-to-end. Backend: 39/39 pytest passed (test_ec8c_schedule.py, test_ec8c_employee_portal.py, plus 8b regression) + manual curl verification of cross-portal 403s, timesheet field-hiding, and dashboard field-hiding — all correct. Frontend: most flows work well (Add Shift, duplicate rejection toast, cancel shift, availability-conflict override flow with amber warning box, Publish, Copy-to-next-week, Employee Portal Access admin page invite/resend/suspend, Employee Detail portal card, nav enablement, Customer Portal regression, Employee Portal Dashboard/Schedule/Timesheet/Announcements/Profile all render and function correctly once authenticated). Found 2 CRITICAL frontend bugs that block core requested flows: (1) Edit Shift dialog does not pre-populate Start/End time inputs, causing an app-crashing 'Invalid time value' RangeError on Save; (2) the Employee Portal magic-link verify page fires the verify API call twice, and since the token is single-use, the 2nd call gets 401 and overrides the successful 1st call's result, showing 'invalid or expired' and blocking login via the verify URL entirely (confirmed via network log: 2 identical POSTs to /api/portal/auth/magic-link/verify). Worked around bug #2 by injecting a valid bearer token into localStorage to continue testing downstream portal pages.

ui_bugs.issue: Start/End time inputs are NOT pre-populated when opening the edit dialog for an existing shift (Date/Title/Location prefill correctly, but Start/End render as empty '--:-- --'). Clicking Save without manually re-entering both times throws an uncaught RangeError: Invalid time value at combineDateTime -> toStartEnd -> submit, crashing the page with the CRA red error overlay. Reproduced twice consistently (Dev Owner's Mon shift and a TEST_Employee's Tue shift).

The verify page calls POST /api/portal/auth/magic-link/verify TWICE on mount (likely a missing guard against React double-effect invocation). Since the magic-link token is single-use and gets consumed on the first successful call, the second call returns 401 'Invalid or expired token', and the UI surfaces this failure to the user (redirects to login with 'That sign-in link is invalid or expired'), even though the first call actually succeeded server-side. This blocks the entire magic-link login flow via the verify URL — the exact flow described in the test task ('lands the user on the Employee Portal dashboard'). Confirmed via captured network requests: 2 identical POSTs fired for the same page load.
After clocking in, the displayed text reads 'Clocked in at 09:03 AM0' — a stray trailing '0' character appears after the formatted time.

ui_bugs.component: React hydration warning (app-wide, seen on both portal apps)
ui_bugs.issue:
Console warning: '
cannot be a descendant of

' — a Badge (renders a

) is nested inside a

in EmployeePortalApp.jsx (~line 195, timesheet_status badge). Causes a hydration warning, cosmetic/code-quality issue, not currently breaking rendering.

Date/Start/End fields use native browser date/time inputs instead of the shadcn date/time picker used elsewhere (consistent with a previously-reported cosmetic finding on EmployeeDetailPage's Hire date field from iteration_11).

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix TeamSchedulePage Edit Shift dialog to pre-populate Start/End time inputs from the existing shift's start_at/end_at (likely a date-object vs string formatting mismatch feeding the time <input>). Also add a guard so submit doesn't crash with an uncaught RangeError if start/end happen to be empty — show a validation message instead.

CRITICAL: Fix the Employee Portal magic-link verify page to only call the verify API once (guard the effect with a ref/flag against React StrictMode double-invocation, or dedupe via an in-flight promise). This currently makes every magic-link login attempt via the browser fail on the very first click.
LOW: Remove stray '0' character after clock-in time text on the Employee Portal dashboard Time Clock card.

LOW: Fix Badge-inside-

HTML nesting in EmployeePortalApp.jsx to remove the hydration warning.

Optional polish: replace native date/time inputs on the Add/Edit Shift dialog with the shadcn date/time picker components for visual consistency (same class of issue previously flagged on EmployeeDetailPage).
critical_code_review_comments: portal_employee.py correctly reuses time_clock_service/timesheet_service directly (thin wrappers), and _public_timesheet_view / _public_employee_view correctly strip estimated_gross_cents and hourly_rate_cents before returning to the employee-facing API — verified empirically via curl that neither field ever appears in /api/portal/employee/timesheet/weekly or /api/portal/employee/dashboard responses.

deps_portal.py's require_employee_portal_permission is solid defense-in-depth: re-checks portal_type on every request AND re-checks the linked Employee's active status live (not just at token-mint time), with an audit_events record on denial — good design.

The Edit Shift prefill bug and the double-verify-call bug both look like straightforward React state-initialization / effect-guard bugs (not backend/data issues) — should be quick, localized fixes in TeamSchedulePage.jsx and EmployeePortalApp.jsx respectively.
Availability-conflict override flow (409 -> inline amber warning -> override reason -> Save anyway -> override badge in grid) is implemented cleanly and matches spec exactly — no issues found there.

success_rate.backend: 100% (39/39 pytest, 8 additional manual curl checks all correct)
success_rate.frontend: ~80% of requested flows work correctly; 2 critical bugs block Edit Shift and Magic-Link Verify specifically

test_credentials: Used dev-login (AUTH_DEV_BYPASS) + POST /api/dev-tools/ensure-dev-employee for staff; POST /api/employee-portal/{id}/invite + POST /api/dev-tools/mint-employee-portal-login for employee portal raw tokens, matching /app/memory/test_credentials.md exactly. To continue testing Employee Portal UI pages despite the verify-page bug, injected a valid bearer JWT (obtained via curl POST /api/portal/auth/magic-link/verify) directly into localStorage key sg_employee_portal_token before navigating to /portal/employee.
seed_data_creation: Added one availability 'unavailable' block for TEST_Employee_677ae44c (2026-07-22) and gave that employee an email (test.677ae44c@example.com) via PATCH to enable Invite testing. Created several TEST-week shifts (2026-07-14, 2026-07-20 override, 2026-07-22 override) across weeks 2026-07-11 and 2026-07-18 in the Dev Shop tenant; some cancelled during testing, others left published. Not cleaned up — dev tenant is disposable.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend for Phase 8c is fully solid (39/39 pytest + manual verification of cross-portal security, field redaction). Frontend has 2 CRITICAL bugs that need main agent fixes before this phase can be considered done: (1) TeamSchedulePage Edit Shift dialog doesn't prefill Start/End times -> crashes on Save; (2) EmployeePortalApp verify page double-fires the magic-link verify call, breaking every login attempt via the actual /portal/employee/verify?t=... URL (the intended real-world flow). All other flows (Add Shift, duplicate/overlap rejection, cancel, availability override, publish, copy-week, Employee Portal Access admin page, Employee Detail portal card, nav enablement, Dashboard/Schedule/Timesheet/Announcements/Profile once authenticated, Customer Portal regression, cross-portal 403s) verified working correctly. Once main agent fixes the 2 critical bugs, retest: (a) edit-shift-then-save-without-touching-times, and (b) actual click-through of a freshly minted magic link from /portal/employee/login all the way to landing on the dashboard (not via localStorage injection).
rca_of_the_issue: Bug 1 (Edit Shift blank times): The edit dialog's initial form state for start/end is very likely derived from an ISO datetime string via a date-parsing helper that fails silently for the edit case (works for Add since there's no existing value to parse), leaving the <input type='time'> value empty; submitting then feeds undefined/empty strings into a Date constructor which throws RangeError: Invalid time value inside combineDateTime. Bug 2 (double verify call): classic React 18 StrictMode/dev double-effect-invocation issue — the verify page's useEffect (or equivalent onMount fetch) isn't guarded with a ref/flag to prevent a second fire, and because the backend token is intentionally single-use, the second of the two identical calls always fails with 401, and that failure is what the UI ends up displaying to the user regardless of the first call's success.
